title: Bridge Essay: Rethinking the bodily self: evidence from the enfacement illusion in women at ri

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Physical De-solidification** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Body as process not substance — neuroplasticity reveals the self-model as continuously updated prediction.

**Rethinking the bodily self: evidence from the enfacement illusion in women at risk for eating disorders.**. Journal of eating disorders.. PMID: 41437122. DOI: 10.1186/s40337-025-01461-1. BACKGROUND: Bodily illusion research has demonstrated that altered bodily-self-perception in eating disorders (EDs) may be linked to abnormalities in the integration of sensory bodily signals. Experiencing bodily illusions can also temporarily reduce body image disturbance. Whether similar alterations in multisensory integration processes extend to self-face representation and whether face-based multisensory illusions can reduce face or body image disturbance remains unclear.
